title: Changing the Future of Electronics with Zhenan Bao
text:
I'm going to tell you about a type of
electronics that we're trying to build
in my laboratory we call skiing inspired
Electronics so I want you to look at
your skin and feel your fingertip
well our skin is capable of sensing the
surroundings and send information to our
brain so that's what we want to mimic
using our electronics and skin is
incredibly complex so if we are able to
do that using science and engineering
then that means potentially we can
change how human interface with
electronics but also we hope that could
allow us to better understand our health
and be able to monitor our own health
so we have Electronics everywhere
information all around us and our
Electronics is already so Advanced but
yet if we look at today's diagnosis and
monitoring devices and medical devices
that are being used they either don't
provide enough information or they don't
really compatible with our human body
they're rigid and a bulky and very
difficult to be broadly used and really
be used to especially inform us about
our health
there's a big need to move Beyond
Precision medicine which is to come up
with personalized treatment when a
disease is discovered but to move
towards Precision Health which is to
monitor our house
continuously and be able to discover a
potential issue before that happens so
imagine the engines for airplane we want
to discover an issue before a
catastrophic failure happens so that's
exactly what we want to do for human
health there's also a big need to really
understand how all the different signals
information electrical information
chemical information that really governs
human health and how they really impact
each other and this is uh the
information that's generated at cellular
level which is only tens of Micron
smaller than the width of human hair all
the ways spread over even meter scale so
that means we need electronics that are
able to measure information at a high
resolution over large area and also in
human without interrupting any of our
everyday life but we cannot do that
because today's electronics are still
made with a rigid and Brito materials
and what we envision is to have
electronics that are comfortable
invisible imperceptible but compatible
and be able to measure information
autonomously so that's the electronic
skin that we are trying to build
as a chemist I wanted to build this from
ground up from the materials because the
electronic materials that we need to
build these things they did not exist
before trained as a chemist and now a
chemical engineer we need to think about
how we can come up with materials that
are as soft as jelly but half the
electronic property as good as those
used in our integrated circuits that are
being used so that's the big challenge
we try to overcome and also from
molecular level these molecules that
from the conventional wisdom in
designing either electronic molecules or
rubber band type of materials their
requirements are completely opposite
from each other so we have to figure out
how we can come up with a creative way
to design this new generation of
materials there address the challenges
of one case we want molecules to be
rigid to conduct electricity on the
other hand for rubber band we want them
to be very very flexible
so we had a discovery that was really
important now for us to be able to
really put skins by the electronics on
the map for new type of electronics that
is we found ways to make these molecules
and not only be able to conduct
electricity effectively but we found
also a way to make them into bundles so
that they can not only transport
electrons more efficiently more
efficiently but also at the same time we
can incorporate things such as
stretchability
biodegradability self-healing properties
all into the same electronic materials
whether they are semiconductors or
conductors now we have a variety of such

and uh since I like to make molecules I
like to Showcase this molecule here to
make the conducting polymer stretchable
we design special molecules that have
this ring-like
shape molecules surrounding them so that
when we pull these molecules these are
circular like molecules can slide around
and that's how we try to design these
materials to be able to tolerate
mechanical stretching and dissipate the
mechanical energy without breaking so
with this kind of electronic material we
start to have electronics that we can
measure information not only from human
body also measure creatures that were
not possible to be measured before for
example this octopus which moves around
but we are able to capture the muscle
electrical signal from such kind of
creature
and then with all the materials coming
together because we need a variety of
them to build functional electronic

imagine the screen that you work with it
can pop up or change shape when you
touch it so this could potentially be
used to image our blood vessel when
placing the sensors directly onto our
skin
let me show you some examples of the
things we're building in the laboratory
this is a new type of skin sensor that's
made of biodegradable and biocompatible
materials we spray on the material
directly onto the human hand and then we
train it a few times then it will be
able to recognize different objects for
example these
cones structure or cylinder structure or
different kinds of shape it can also be
trained just with a few keystrokes to be
able to this is a virtual keyboard it's
typing on and then the algorithm is
recognizing the information from the
typing these are little mouse that's
carrying the sensors that we built the
sensors and also the electrodes are
continued continuously generating
electrical signal to stimulate the

monitoring uh the the progress of wound
healing so with this kind of device so
we're not only able to make the wound
heal faster and have lesser scarring but
also we can now start using this to
understand what kind of genes get
upregulated when we are able to perform
this kind of treatment
this is uh something I'm very passionate
about that is to develop wearables that
can allow us to monitor our own mental

still slightly rigid the second
generation we hope to be much more skin
like they're incorporating various
different sensors to monitor the state
of our Mental Health
this is a a newest sensor we develop you
heard about dopamine in first lecture
this sensor measures dopamine
simultaneously in the brain and also in
the intestine so we actually give this
mouse chocolate then we measured
dopamine generation when the mouse saw
the chocolate and then when the
chocolate get to the intestine we were
able to measure a spike of the serotonin
that's generated in the intestine and
this kind of experiments never have been
done before because the sensors
typically traditionally they were too
rigid to put into a behaving animal to
be able to perform such measurement
not only we're making the sensors to be
soft skin like but also the integrated
circuits they are important for the
signal processing and wireless
communication so we're making our way
towards really making the rigid wafer
like integrate circuits now to skin like
integrated circuits
we're making some progress towards
translating our first generation of
mimicking sense of touch sensors to now
hopefully a product that can be will be

infant continuous blood pressure
monitoring so those are the skin
inspired Electronics we're thinking
about and finally I'd like to thank my
research team and it's really their
creativity and hard work that brought us
to where we are today and so much more

without a boundary there are many many

to thank them and thank you very much
